note: Behavioral detection rules - NOT CVE ID string matching

rules.title: Sequential HTTP 401 to 200 Authentication Bypass
rules.description: Detects HTTP authentication bypass patterns where a 401 Unauthorized response is immediately followed by a 200 OK on a protected endpoint, indicating potential authentication bypass exploitation.
falsepositives: Legitimate users with cached credentials re-authenticating

rules.title: JWT None Algorithm Authentication Bypass
rules.description: Detects use of 'none' algorithm in JWT tokens which bypasses signature verification on vulnerable implementations.
falsepositives: Intentionally unsigned tokens in test environments

rules.title: Ransomware Shadow Copy and Backup Deletion Chain
rules.description: Detects the combination of shadow copy deletion, backup catalog deletion, and boot recovery disable commands executed in sequence - the standard ransomware pre-encryption preparation routine.
falsepositives: Legitimate system administrators performing backup maintenance

rules.title: Mass File Extension Rename - Ransomware Encryption
rules.description: Detects rapid bulk file renaming to unknown extensions indicative of ransomware encryption activity.

rules.title: Ransomware Service and Process Termination
rules.description: Detects bulk service and process termination targeting backup, database, and AV services - standard ransomware preparation to maximize encryption impact.
falsepositives: System administrators stopping services for maintenance

rules.title: Office Application Spawning Scripting Engine
rules.description: Detects Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) spawning scripting engines or command interpreters, indicating macro or embedded script execution from a potentially malicious document.
falsepositives: Legitimate macros in trusted document templates

rules.title: HTML Smuggling File Written to Downloads
rules.description: Detects HTML smuggling technique where a browser writes an executable or archive to the Downloads folder as triggered by visiting a malicious web page.

rules.title: Process Injection via WriteProcessMemory and CreateRemoteThread
rules.description: Detects classic process injection technique using WriteProcessMemory followed by CreateRemoteThread - used by Cobalt Strike, Meterpreter, and numerous malware loaders.

rules.title: Registry Run Key Persistence by Suspicious Process
rules.description: Detects malware persistence via Windows Registry run keys created by non-standard parent processes.
falsepositives: Legitimate software setting startup entries from user profile

rules.title: C2 Beaconing - Regular Interval Outbound Connection
rules.description: Detects regular-interval outbound connections from non-browser processes indicative of C2 beaconing behavior.
falsepositives: Legitimate applications using rundll32 for network operations

rules.title: Web Server Process Spawning Interactive Shell
rules.description: Detects web server processes spawning command shells or interpreters, a strong indicator of successful web application exploitation (RCE, SQLi, LFI to RCE, web shell execution).
falsepositives: Web applications legitimately spawning shell for system integration

rules.title: Exchange Server OWA Web Shell Execution
rules.description: Detects web shell execution on Microsoft Exchange Server OWA paths, indicative of ProxyLogon/ProxyShell or similar Exchange exploitation.

rules.title: Log4Shell JNDI Exploitation Attempt
rules.description: Detects Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) exploitation attempts via JNDI injection strings in HTTP headers or request parameters.
